meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no project ideas. Learn to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship high-value client projects at record speed.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing inspiration and start building infrastructure. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you transform boilerplate into a repeatable product engine. Use Antigravity to scaffold architecture and Claude Code to iterate features, turning the 'no project ideas' trap into a high-velocity, client-ready development workflow that scales.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the freelancer’s silent assassin. You sit down to build, but the weight of 'what to build' paralyzes your momentum, turning potential income into wasted hours of overthinking. The market doesn't pay for ideas; it pays for execution, reliability, and the speed at which you can deliver a polished, production-ready solution.

The secret isn't a flash of genius—it's a system. By adopting a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you stop reinventing authentication and database schemas and start shipping value. This guide bridges the gap between 'I have no project' and 'I just deployed,' using the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code to automate your path to profit.

steps.title: Define the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Establish the foundational database schema and auth requirements that every SaaS needs, ensuring you don't waste time on repetitive boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a SaaS starter kit architecture including Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe integration. Define the core user database schema and role-based access control.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate base project structure with nextjs and supabase'

steps.title: Implement Authentication Flow
steps.description: Secure your app immediately. Use the starter kit to implement robust OAuth and email sign-in flows without manual configuration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure authentication module with Supabase Auth, including password recovery and social login providers for Google and GitHub.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement auth flow with email and google provider'

steps.title: Setup Payment Infrastructure
steps.description: Connect Stripe to your starter kit to ensure your projects are monetizable from day one, handling subscriptions and webhooks automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Stripe billing webhooks and subscription plans within the existing SaaS starter kit architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup stripe billing webhook listener and subscription service'

steps.title: Develop Core Feature Module
steps.description: Build the unique value proposition for your specific client project, using the starter kit's standardized API routes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CRUD API route for a new resource named 'ProjectTask' that follows the existing project's architectural patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate crud api endpoints for ProjectTask resource'

steps.title: UI Components and Theming
steps.description: Apply a professional design system using Tailwind CSS to ensure your project looks like it was built by a senior engineering team.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a responsive dashboard layout using Shadcn UI components that matches the established brand theme.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add dashboard layout with shadcn components'

pro_tips: Always build your SaaS starter kit to be 'database-agnostic' so you can swap Supabase for PostgreSQL or Drizzle at a moment's notice.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests simultaneously with the feature code to maintain 90%+ coverage automatically.
Automate your deployment pipeline using GitHub Actions to ensure every push to the main branch is instantly live on production.
Keep your UI components in a private npm package or a shared 'components' folder to reuse across all your different client projects.
Prioritize 'feature flagging' early; it allows you to test new ideas in production without breaking the live client experience.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is fully configured, clone it for your next three client proposals. Use the time saved on boilerplate to focus exclusively on high-conversion UI/UX and unique business logic, effectively doubling your hourly rate.
content.conversion_block: Stop settling for project paralysis and start shipping high-value SaaS products that pay the bills. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
